word.item: ‎‎I want to see you; I want you to come you is fem. here
I love you; I'm glad when I see you
a crack is open, spread apart
a crack is open, spread apart
house - walls, walls of a house
he sent him a challenge to fight
they had (in their feet) they were sitting
they wanted to see how they would turn
in the claws they grasped and they were sitting
they were still hitting one another
crawfish (a special kind, having big pinchers)
crab (one way of saying it, not the old form, however)
our mouth [not quite right, but understandable]
I'm going want to hit you
I'm going want to hit him
I? you want to hit me
you got tired [some doubt in inf.'s mind.]
I throw it over my shoulder
when he finished making his hickory stick
he towed (carried) it on his back
he brought sticks on his back
she's going to peel & split it
(she told him) to come and eat
he got to the place where he had been sitting
she tried to slip off with her elbow
she was trying to hit him
she was trying to hit him
she was trying to hit him
when she tried to bruise him
she fell on her knees (every time)
tomorrow he's going to get cane
tomorrow he's going to get cane
he stood at the end of the step
he broke the arrow in two
he roll he got out (to the water)
he roll he got out (the the water)
he stacked them on his back
he put, stood them (on his back)
picked them up and ate them
I'm going to throw you in
bring with me I'm going to look
the gaspigou is in the water
the ant is walking i.e. crawling
the lizard (with stripe down back) is lying
the side of the house lies
